a: Two endpoints are logically equivalent if they have the same service type (publish vs subscribe) with the same name, and refer to the same device.
Add entitlements and declare your app’s services.
Communicate with nearby devices over a secure, high-throughput, low-latency connection by using Wi-Fi Aware.
Make outgoing and accept incoming secure connections with paired devices.
The underling quality-of-service (QoS) the Wi-Fi layer uses to transmit data packets from a connection over the air.
A quality-of-service (QoS) type that provides high throughput for delay-tolerant, noninteractive data transfers of any size.
A default quality-of-service (QoS) type that provides high throughput for data transfers of any size.
A quality-of-service (QoS) type that provides low-latency for moderate throughput flows.
A quality-of-service (QoS) type that provides very low-latency for low-throughput flows.
A structure that checks the host device’s supported features and capabilities.
Features that your app’s current host device can support.
Indicates that the host supports Wi-Fi Aware.
Provides access to the Wi-Fi Aware-specific configuration and information that underlies a given `Network/NetworkConnection`.
The endpoint of a Wi-Fi Aware connection.
An error in Wi-Fi Aware.
The optional details describing the failed connection.
The optional details describing the missing resources.
The optional details describing the terminated connection.
The optional details describing the device that’s invalid.
The optional details describing the unavailable device.
The optional details describing the missing entitlement.
The optional details describing the error.
The optional details describing the lack of paired devices.
The optional details describing what resources are lacking.
The optional details describing the timed out publisher.
The optional details describing the service that’s publishing.
The optional details describing the service that’s subscribing.
The optional details that describe the app service wasn’t declared.
The optional details describing the timed out subscriber.
The optional details describing the unavailability of Wi-Fi Aware on the device.
An error that occurs if the service is unable to connect.
An error that occurs due to an idle or unused connection.
An error that occurs if the connection was terminated.
An error that occurs if your app specifies a paired device it doesn’t have access to.
An error that occurs if a device is no longer available.
An error that occurs if your app is missing the entitlement needed for the requested operation.
A general error.
An error that occurs if your app doesn’t have access to any paired devices.
An error that occurs if the radio lacks resources.
An error that occurs due to publisher timeout.
An error that occurs if a new publisher or `Network/NetworkListener` can’t be created.
An error that occurs if a new subscriber or `Network/NetworkBrowser` can’t be created.
An error that occurs if your app didn’t declare the necessary services.
An error that occurs due to subscriber timeout.
An error that occurs if Wi-Fi Aware isn’t supported on the device.
A known Wi-Fi Aware device that your app can connect to.
A dictionary holding a snapshot of currently paired devices accessible and known to your app.
A sequence that vends updates to a paired device list, as the list changes.
An iterator for the sequence of devices.
A dictionary holding a snapshot of currently paired devices accessible to your app.
The type of error that the sequence can produce.
A dictionary holding a snapshot of currently paired devices accessible to your app.
A type of value that uniquely identifies the paired device.
A collection of unauthenticated information the system receives from a device before it’s paired for the first time.
Parameters configuring a Wi-Fi Aware data path connection.
A representation of the current Wi-Fi Aware path.
The performance forecast for a connection setup to the remote device.
The performance mode that indicates what performance criterion to prioritize.
A mode that prioritizes throughput, power, and support for other concurrent Wi-Fi use cases and devices.
A mode that prioritizes latency at the expense of throughput, power, and other concurrent Wi-Fi use cases and devices.
The current performance state of the data path.
A report of the transmit latency to the specified peer.
A service, hosted by your app, that remote devices can connect to.
The type of value that uniquely identifies the service.
Configures a network listener to publish a service over Wi-Fi Aware and accept incoming connections from paired devices.
The structure that configures the Wi-Fi Aware publisher operation that the network listener performs.
The parameter that sets the initial Wi-Fi Aware data path configuration for any devices that are connected.
The structure that determines the devices to connect to.
A protocol that defines a service that a device can publish or subscribe to.
A high-entropy shared secret unique to this network connection.
A unique value that is specific to your App and the use case a given connection will perform, which diversifies the generated secret so that it is unique to your App and connection.
An enumeration of methods that can be used to derive a shared secret, as specified in the Wi-Fi Aware standard.
An object that uniquely identifies a particular network protocol and the pairing hanshake it uses for authentication.
A service your app discovers on remote devices and can connect to.
The type of value that uniquely identifies the service.
The structure that configures a network browser to subscribe to a Wi-Fi Aware service and make outgoing connections to paired devices.
The structure that configures the Wi-Fi Aware subscriber operation the network browser performs.
The structure that determines the devices to connect to.
A result for each discovered Wi-Fi Aware device.
Securely pair and connect to external devices over peer-to-peer Wi-Fi.
The Wi-Fi quality-of-service (QoS) category that this latency data tracks.
Makes additional network connections over a preexisting Wi-Fi Aware data path without publishing or advertising services over Wi-Fi Aware.
Provides a snapshot of all the paired devices known to your app.
Provides a snapshot of all the paired devices known to your app.
The property that includes all paired devices that your app has access to.
The property that includes all paired devices that your app has access to.
A dictionary of all publishable services declared by your app, indexed by service name.
A dictionary of all subscribable services declared by your app, indexed by service name.
A dictionary of all services declared by your app, indexed by service name.
The average per-packet transmit latency measured for this flow’s peers.
A string that provides a unique value specific to your app.
Configures the provided parameters for a Wi-Fi Aware publish operation and connection.
Returns the parameters to use to configure the Wi-Fi Aware subscriber and the subsequent connection.
Subscribes to the provided service over Wi-Fi, providing browse results for connecting to the specified paired devices if available.
Publishes the provided service over Wi-Fi, enabling connections from the specified paired devices.
Fetches a one-time snapshot of all paired devices that are currently known and  accessible to your app.
The secret data that is derived by both this device and the remote device for this connection.
The property that configures default parameters that prioritize bulk throughput, power consumption, and other concurrent Wi-Fi use cases.
The property that configures default parameters that prioritize bulk throughput, power consumption, and other concurrent Wi-Fi use cases.
The method used to derive the shared secret on both sides of the connection, as per the Wi-Fi Aware standard.

Derive a unique, high-entropy shared secret for this network connection, which can be used to pair and setup security for higher layer network protocols like TLS or IPSec without additional user action or entropy input.
A string description of the pairing information.
A description of the service.
A string description of the service.
A string description of the publisher.
The description of the object.
The remote device that is, or can be, connected.
A property that indicates a cumulative duration data path is connected on this Wi-Fi Aware path.
The Wi-Fi Aware endpoint that’s connected.
Compute unique hash of this object.
A stable ID that can be used to identify this publishable service.
A stable ID that can be used to identify this subscribable service.
A stable ID that you can use to uniquely identify a device.
Creates a new custom context that provides a unique string.
Creates a new custom context that provides unique data.
Creates a custom protocol with the provided unique string.
Creates a custom protocol name using the provided unique string.
Initializes the parameters with defaults.
Derive a shared secret to bootstrap IPSec, using the resulting shared secret as the IPSec Pre-Shared Key.
A Boolean value that determines whether the devices are listening for an application service.
Derive a shared secret using the KDF-HASH-256 method defined in the WiFi Aware 5.0 draft standard.
The estimated normalized ratio of the local Wi-Fi Infrastructure throughput capacity and the local Wi-Fi Infrastructure throughput ceiling, if a Wi-Fi Aware connection is setup to the remote device.
The estimated average throughput capacity of the local device for a connection setup to the remote device.
The estimated normalized ratio of the local throughput capacity and the local throughput ceiling.

The highest throughput a connection from the local device to the remote device is capable of under ideal conditions, given the hardware capabilities of the local device. The actual throughput achieved can be lower in the presence of other active connections.
The time the forecast was generated, using a local monotonically increasing clock.
The time the report was generated, using a local monotonically increasing clock.
Makes an asynchronous iterator that provides successive device snapshots when the list of paired devices known to the app changes.
Makes a descriptor that can create a network browser for a Wi-Fi Aware subscribe operation.
Creates a connectable Wi-Fi Aware endpoint from a browse result.
Includes only paired devices matching the provided live filter predicate.
Includes only paired devices matching the provided live filter predicate.
The maximum number of unique devices your app can connect to simultaneously.
The maximum number of unique services your app can publish simultaneously.
The maximum number of unique services your app can simultaneously subscribe to.
The human-readable model name of the physical device as a person can understand it.
The full name of the service, as sent over the air.
The full name of the service, as sent over the air.
The full name of the service, as sent over the air.
The user-provided name of the device as a string, or `nil` if not available.
Returns the next dictionary in the sequence.
The unauthenticated information provided by the device before a person pairs it for the first time.
The human-readable name of the physical device as a person sees it during initial pairing.
The current performance metrics for the Wi-Fi Aware data path.
The forecasted performance for connections setup to the remote device, per WAPerformanceMode.
The initial performance configuration of the data path when connected.
The publishable service that is, or can be, connected.
Parameters that prioritize latency at the expense of throughput, power consumption, and other concurrent Wi-Fi use cases and devices.
The property that configures parameters that prioritize latency at the expense of throughput, power consumption, and other concurrent Wi-Fi use cases.
Includes only the preselected paired devices in the provided dictionary.
Includes only the preselected paired devices in the provided list.
Includes only the preselected paired devices in the provided list.
Includes only the preselected paired devices in the provided dictionary.
The service data for the Wi-Fi Aware publish operation.
The estimated signal strength of the remote device.
The current signal strength of the remote device.
The subscribable service that is, or can be, connected.
A property that returns a set of supported features, or an empty set if the current platform doesn’t support Wi-Fi Aware.
The current estimated average throughput capacity of the data path, given the current radio conditions and concurrent Wi-Fi use cases.
The current normalized ratio of the throughput capacity and throughput ceiling.
The highest throughput the connection is capable of under ideal conditions, given the hardware capabilities of both devices.
The time the forecast was generated.
The time that the framework generates the report.
Derive a shared secret to bootstrap TLS, using the resulting shared secret as a TLS Pre-Shared Key.
The measured transmit latency per access category that is in use with the remote devices and that the system can measure.
The highest forecasted packet latency due to this device’s unavailability.
Includes only new devices the user pairs via DeviceDiscoveryUI’s `DevicePairingView()`.
Includes only devices the user selects or pairs in DeviceDiscoveryUI’s `DevicePicker()`.
The human-readable name of the manufacturer or vendor of the physical device as a person would understand it.
